### safe_relative_to

>  safe_relative_to (path:pathlib.Path, base:pathlib.Path,
>                        fallback_base:Optional[pathlib.Path]=None)

*Safely compute relative path, with fallback for paths outside base.*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>

<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>path</td>
<td>Path</td>
<td></td>
<td>The path to make relative.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>base</td>
<td>Path</td>
<td></td>
<td>The primary base directory to make path relative to.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>fallback_base</td>
<td>Optional</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>If path is not under base, try this as fallback.<br>If None and path
is not under base, returns path.name.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>str</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>Relative path string, or just the filename if path is
outside all bases.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Environment Files

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L200"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### env_file

>  env_file (project:pathlib.Path|None=None, scoped:bool=True,
>                envs_dir:pathlib.Path|None=None,
>                lib_name_override:str|None=None)

*Resolve the environment YAML path with sensible fallbacks.*

Searches for environment files in this priority order:

1.  `{envs_dir}/env.{lib_name}.yml` (scoped, custom dir)
2.  `{envs_dir}/env.yml` (unscoped, custom dir)
3.  `{project}/env.{lib_name}.yml` (scoped, project root)
4.  `{project}/env.yml` (unscoped, project root)
5.  `{project}/../Environments/env.{lib_name}.yml` (sibling dir)
6.  `{project}/../Environments/env.yml` (sibling dir)

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>pathlib.Path | None</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>The nbdev project root. Defaults to current directory.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>scoped</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>If True, prefer <code>env.{lib_name}.yml</code> over
<code>env.yml</code>.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>envs_dir</td>
<td>pathlib.Path | None</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Custom directory to search first for environment files.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>lib_name_override</td>
<td>str | None</td>
<td>None</td>
<td>Library name for scoped files. Defaults to lib_name from
settings.ini.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Path</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td><strong>The first existing environment file, or the first candidate
if none exist.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L250"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

*Ascend from the given path to find the root of an nbdev project.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L298"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### require_project

>  require_project ()

*Get the currently active project Path, defaulting to cwd if valid.*

Resolution order: 1. If CURRENT_PROJECT is explicitly set, return it 2.
If current working directory is an nbdev project, use it 3. If cwd is
inside an nbdev project, use the project root 4. Otherwise raise
RuntimeError

This enables single-project mode (just cd into your project) while still
supporting multi-project workflows via set_project().

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L339"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### resolve_selector

>  resolve_selector (selector:Optional[str])

*Resolve a project selector string to an nbdev project Path.*

Selector formats: - `None` -\> returns CURRENT_PROJECT (if set,
otherwise raises error) - `'@alias'` or `'alias:NAME'` -\> looks up the
alias in bookmarks - any other string -\> treated as a filesystem path

Args: selector: Project selector string.

Returns: Resolved project root path.

Raises: RuntimeError: If selector doesn’t resolve to a valid nbdev
project.

## Notebook Iteration

### Cache

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L388"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### clear_notebook_cache

>  clear_notebook_cache ()

*Clear the notebook listing cache.*

Removes all cached notebook listings. Call this when notebooks are added
or removed to force a fresh scan on next access.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L399"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

>  iter_notebooks_uncached (project:pathlib.Path)

*Internal: collect notebooks from nbs/ and tutorials/ without caching.*

### iter_notebooks

>  iter_notebooks (project:pathlib.Path, use_cache:bool=True)

*Iterate over all notebooks in the project.*

Yields notebooks from nbs/ and tutorials/ directories in sorted order,
excluding `.ipynb_checkpoints` folders.

Results are cached for 30 seconds to improve performance on repeated
calls.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 6%" />
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
<col style="width: 34%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>

<th><strong>Default</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Path</td>
<td></td>
<td>Path to the nbdev project root.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>use_cache</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Whether to use caching (default True). Set to False to force
refresh.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Iterable</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Path</td>
<td></td>
<td>Path to the nbdev project root.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>use_cache</td>
<td>bool</td>
<td>True</td>
<td>Whether to use caching (default True). Set to False to force
reload.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Dict</strong></td>
<td></td>
<td>**The ‘d’ dict from _modidx.py containing module/symbol info.**</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L677"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### clear_modidx_cache

>  clear_modidx_cache ()

\*Clear the \_modidx.py cache.\*

Removes all cached modidx data. Call this when \_modidx.py changes to
force a fresh parse on next access.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L660"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### modidx_path

>  modidx_path (project:pathlib.Path)

\*Return the path to \_modidx.py for the project.\*

<table>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>project</td>
<td>Path</td>
<td>Path to the nbdev project root.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Path</strong></td>
<td><strong>Resolved path to
<code>{lib}/_modidx.py</code>.</strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## Export

## with_project Decorator

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L767"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### with_project

>  with_project (fn:Callable[...,Dict[str,Any]])

*Decorator that handles project resolution boilerplate for tool
functions.*

Wraps a function that takes a Path as its first argument, converting the
optional string `project` parameter to a resolved Path. If resolution
fails, returns `{'ok': False, 'error': ...}` instead of raising.

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 9%" />
<col style="width: 38%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th></th>
<th><strong>Type</strong></th>
<th><strong>Details</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>fn</td>
<td>Callable</td>
<td>Function with signature
<code>(p: Path, ...) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]</code><br>where <code>p</code>
is the resolved project path.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><strong>Returns</strong></td>
<td><strong>Callable</strong></td>
<td><strong>Wrapped function with signature
<code>(project: Optional[str] = None, ...) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]</code></strong></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

## MCP Configuration Paths

Provider-specific configuration paths for AI tools.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L803"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### get_vscode_user_dir

>  get_vscode_user_dir ()

*Get VS Code user settings directory.*

Returns platform-specific path: - macOS: ~/Library/Application
Support/Code/User - Windows: %APPDATA%/Code/User - Linux:
~/.config/Code/User

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/s01st/nbdev-mcp/blob/main/nbdev_mcp/utils/paths.py#L819"
